title: Electronic Commerce Professional Services: 1998 Practice Summary
author: Aberdeen Group
abstract: A 1998 practice summary analyzing the market for professional services supporting electronic commerce deployment. The report defines four categories of EC professional services providers (OEMs, SSPs, IPSPs, EDI/VAN integrators), identifies key implementation challenges (skills shortages, integration complexity, standards immaturity), and profiles 11 major service providers including IBM, HP, Digital, Microsoft, Oracle, SAP, Cambridge Technology Partners, EDS, GEIS, Sterling Commerce, and Harbinger. Key Aberdeen finding: enterprises are turning to outside service providers because internal IS resources lack EC skills.
importance_rationale: Documents the emergence of e-commerce professional services as a distinct market category in 1998; provides the first structured taxonomy of EC services providers; based on IS manager interviews making it a primary source on enterprise EC adoption drivers.
relevance_rationale: Professional services for digital commerce integration remain a major market; Aberdeen's four-category taxonomy (OEM/SSP/IPSP/EDI-VAN) maps to modern SaaS partner ecosystems; skill shortage observations proved prophetic and persistent.
prescience_rationale: Key predictions verified: EDI/VAN providers were eclipsed by internet commerce; ERP providers (SAP Oracle) proved to be the best entry point to enterprise EC; skills shortage persisted and worsened; B2B internet commerce growth far exceeded forecasts.
